title: Wholesome Egyptian Egg Dumplings
description: A fresh, crumbly, low-fat Egyptian dish featuring golden egg rolls with herbs and vegetables, perfect for a cozy lunch.
instructions: Beat the eggs in a large bowl until fully blended.
Whisk together the flour, baking powder, salt, and ground cumin until well combined.
Add the dry mixture to the eggs and stir until a smooth batter forms.
Stir in the milk, parsley, tomato, cucumber, and lemon juice until evenly distributed.
Heat the olive oil in a non‑stick skillet over medium heat.
Drop spoonfuls of the batter into the skillet, forming small rounds about 1/2 inch thick.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges turn golden brown, about 3 minutes.
Flip each dumpling and cook the other side until golden and cooked through, about 2 minutes.
Serve hot with a squeeze of fresh lemon if desired.
